findings.title: Unchecked ERC20 return values can mint unbacked stake receipts or release QWA without burning sQWA

best_agent_match.title: Unchecked ERC20 return values can mint unbacked stake receipts or release QWA without burning sQWA

findings.title: Missed-epoch rewards can be captured by late entrants because `rebase()` only catches up one epoch per call

best_agent_match.title: Missed epochs can be stolen by new stakers because `rebase()` only catches up one epoch per call

findings.title: Predictable epoch boundaries enable just-in-time staking to siphon epoch rewards

best_agent_match.title: Predictable epoch boundaries allow just-in-time staking to siphon each epoch's reward

findings.title: Nominal-amount accounting can undercollateralize the pool if QWA transfers less than the requested amount

best_agent_match.title: Nominal-amount accounting breaks on fee-on-transfer or deflationary QWA and can leave the pool insolvent

rejected_candidates.title: `secondsToNextEpoch()` reverts instead of reporting zero once an epoch is overdue
rejected_candidates.reason: Real underflow in a view function, but it is an off-chain/integration nuisance only and does not create meaningful protocol-level fund loss, lockup, or exploitable on-chain behavior.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing zero-address validation in constructor
rejected_candidates.reason: Deployment-time misconfiguration by the privileged deployer, not a permissionless exploit against a live protocol.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing zero-address validation in setDistributor
rejected_candidates.reason: Setting the distributor to zero only disables distribution until the owner sets a new address again; it is recoverable privileged misconfiguration, not a permanent exploit path.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing reentrancy guard on stake function
rejected_candidates.reason: Speculative. The report does not show a concrete reentrant path that breaks staking invariants beyond assuming malicious core token contracts, and this contract keeps almost no internal accounting to corrupt.

rejected_candidates.title: Unstake with rebase can fail due to insufficient balance check
rejected_candidates.reason: Not an independent bug. The check only fails when the pool is already undercollateralized for some other reason; `unstake(_rebase=true)` is exposing insolvency rather than creating it.

rejected_candidates.title: Anyone can trigger rebase leading to MEV/front-running
rejected_candidates.reason: Public `rebase()` is expected behavior for this design. The only concrete timing harm here is the separate just-in-time staking reward siphon already captured in F-003.

rejected_candidates.title: No access control allows anyone to stake to any address
rejected_candidates.reason: Allowing deposits on behalf of another address is standard and does not by itself create realistic protocol harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Lack of event emission for setDistributor
rejected_candidates.reason: Transparency/operability issue only, not a security finding.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential integer overflow in epoch.end update
rejected_candidates.reason: Solidity 0.8.19 has checked arithmetic, so overflow reverts instead of wrapping, and the scenario is not realistically reachable.
